title: Advanced Kitchen King Masala
description: Advanced Kitchen King Masala is a rich, aromatic spice blend made with roasted whole spices, roasted onion, garlic, and tomato powders. It is perfect for restaurant-style gravies, paneer dishes, dals, and biryanis.

instruction.title: Step 1 – Make Roasted Powders
steps: Onion Powder: Thinly slice 2 medium onions.
Roast on low flame or in a 120°C oven for 45–60 min until golden brown and fully dry.
Cool and grind to a fine powder.
Roast on low heat until dry and slightly golden.
Cool and grind to fine powder.
Tomato Powder: Use 2 medium ripe tomatoes. Slice and dehydrate in oven at 120°C for 4–5 hours until fully dry.

instruction.title: Step 2 – Roast Whole Spices
steps: Heat a pan on low-medium flame.
Add coriander seeds, cumin seeds, fennel seeds, black peppercorns, dry red chilies, cinnamon, cloves, green & black cardamom, bay leaf.
Roast gently for 3–5 minutes until aromatic.

instruction.title: Step 3 – Grind Everything Together
steps: Add powdered spices: nutmeg, mace, dry ginger, turmeric, roasted onion, garlic, and tomato powders.
Grind to a very fine, silky powder.

instruction.title: Step 4 – Store Professionally
steps: Sieve if desired for smooth texture.
Store in an airtight jar away from moisture and sunlight.
Use sparingly—this is intensely flavorful, perfect for restaurant-level curries.

chefTips: Roast powders slowly to avoid bitterness.
Keep roasted powders separate if storing long-term; mix in small batches for freshness.
This masala works beautifully in gravies, paneer dishes, dals, and biryanis.
For an extra umami kick, add a tiny pinch of asafoetida (hing) when cooking.
